A male voice narrates, stating that "Biden lost" and "instead of weapons, Obama went with sanctions," all set against a somber, low-key instrumental score. The scene transitions to a close-up of Joe Biden, his expression somber and reflective, as Evan Osnos's voice begins to explain Obama's perspective on not being elected to focus on Russia and Ukraine. The video then cuts to a color interview with Evan Osnos, identified by on-screen text as 'Evan Osnos, The New Yorker,' speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit room, continuing his analysis about the Vice President's push to "draw a bright line" and declare that "Russian aggression cannot stand." This is followed by a series of intense black-and-white close-ups of Vladimir Putin, his face dramatically illuminated from the side, conveying a stern and penetrating gaze, as Osnos's voice continues, concluding that Obama's internal sentiment was, "Sorry, this is this is just not a top priority for us." The video returns to Putin’s face, now in an extreme close-up, his eyes fixed and unblinking, creating a sense of psychological intensity. The sequence then cuts to Timothy Snyder, identified as 'Author, The Road to Unfreedom,' speaking in a color interview with an earnest and animated expression, explaining that "Putin takes a lesson from Ukraine" when he can "invade a country" with "minimal" reaction. The final frames return to Putin’s eyes, slowly zooming in, heightening the dramatic and confrontational atmosphere, as Snyder concludes his analysis, warning that Putin learns "this kind of thing can work if we gen it up properly," a message not intended to be sent. The somber instrumental score fades out.

event_blocks.summary: The 2016 presidential race is underway, with Vladimir Putin striking at the heart of American democracy by interfering in the election to discredit the U.S. presidency and neutralize the United States as an adversary, as Fiona Hill explains, aiming to tarnish US leadership. Russian hackers breached Democratic Party computer servers, and WikiLeaks displayed DNC email data, with leaks designed to undermine Hillary Clinton's campaign, a direct and assertive Russian effort, according to James Clapper. Stephen Hadley notes the variety and intensity of techniques employed, including misinformation, fake users, Kremlin-supporting trolls, automated bots on Twitter, and targeted ads on Facebook and Google, calculated to hurt Clinton and help Donald Trump. From Putin's perspective, Trump's authoritarianism and desire for a positive Russia relationship made him appealing, as Hadley states, 'he's one of us,' leading to Trump's election as the 45th President. Peter Baker observes that from Putin's viewpoint, the favored candidate won, disrupting America's faith in democracy, while a burning vehicle and protests underscore the ensuing chaos. Trump and Putin met, with Yamiche Alcindor noting Trump's eagerness and Putin's reserved demeanor, as Trump asserted that getting along with Russia is good, though John Bolton believed Putin saw Trump as a fool and easily manipulable. During a two-hour private meeting in Helsinki, Putin, able to read Trump like a violin, later denied election interference at a press conference, a statement Eugene Robinson found stark, and Greg Miller discussed the aftermath. Trump, at the Helsinki podium, stated he saw no reason why it wouldn't be Russia, yet described Putin as extremely strong in his denial, while commentators expressed disbelief, seeing Putin masterfully manipulate Trump, making ropes out of him. Susan Glasser noted Trump acted as an arm of Putin's foreign policy, echoing his talking points on NATO, Ukraine's corruption, and Crimea being Russian, with Trump questioning alliances and stating Ukraine is not a real country, effectively helping Putin reconstitute the Soviet Union. Marie Yovanovitch explained Putin was getting what he needed from Trump, including the humiliation of allies, and likely bet on a second Trump term. However, Joe Biden was elected, and Donald Trump, showing no signs of conceding, declared victory, leading to contested election claims, nervousness, and violence, culminating in protest marches toward the U.S. Capitol.

Server Hardware.description: A close-up of black server components with blinking green indicator lights, implying active data processing or network activity, set in a dark, high-tech environment.

WikiLeaks Website.description: A digital screen displaying the WikiLeaks homepage with the text 'Search the DNC email data' and multiple overlapping email documents, suggesting a data leak, shown in a montage of social media and news content.

Washington Monument.description: A tall, slender obelisk standing prominently in a grassy field, silhouetted against a sunset sky, framed by bare tree branches in the foreground.

FRONTLINE.description: A red rectangular logo with white text reading 'FRONTLINE' in the bottom left corner of the screen, indicating the documentary series producing the segment, visible during Stephen Hadley's interview.

U.S. Capitol Building.description: A grand neoclassical building with a large dome, visible in the background of the protest march, illuminated under a clear sky, serving as the destination for the demonstrators.

James Clapper.description: A bald man wearing a dark suit, white shirt, and patterned tie, speaking with serious, deliberate hand gestures in a dimly lit indoor setting, identified by on-screen text as 'Dir. of National Intelligence, 2010–17'. He speaks in a deep, serious voice, describing the Russian campaign as "the most aggressive, most direct, and most assertive."

Fiona Hill.description: A woman with shoulder-length dark hair, wearing a red collared shirt and a silver necklace, speaking expressively in a dimly lit indoor setting, identified by on-screen text as 'Nat'l. Security Council, 2017–19'. She speaks in a clear, articulate voice with a slight accent, detailing the goals of discrediting elections and the presidency to tarnish US leadership.

The White House.description: A bright, colorful daytime exterior shot of the White House, featuring a central fountain, manicured lawns, and vibrant red tulips in the foreground, under a clear blue sky, visually representing the location in Washington, D.C., which is mentioned by commentators as the place where "concern continues to grow" following the events.

Hillary Clinton.description: A woman with short, styled blonde hair, wearing a dark suit and white blouse, shown in black-and-white footage speaking into a microphone at a podium, with a serious and engaged expression, who is identified by a narrator as the target of efforts 'calculated to hurt'.

Black-and-White Constitutional Backdrop.description: A dark, high-contrast background featuring cursive text from the U.S. Constitution, with stars at the bottom, used as a backdrop for Hillary Clinton and Donald Trump during their black-and-white appearance.

Vladimir Putin.description: A man with a receding hairline, wearing a dark suit, white shirt, and patterned tie, shown in black-and-white close-ups, first seated in an ornate chair with a smirking expression, then in profile with a contemplative gaze, and finally in a direct, intense stare into the camera. He is discussed by a male voiceover and Marie Yovanovitch as someone who benefited from President Trump's actions and was betting on his second term, with a male voiceover later suggesting he 'helped foment' the chaos in America.

Stephen Hadley.description: A man with gray hair and glasses, wearing a dark suit, light blue shirt, and red patterned tie, speaking in a dimly lit room, identified by on-screen text as 'National Security Adviser, 2005–09', with a serious and reflective expression, whose voice is heard discussing the 'variety and intensity of the techniques' employed, and later explaining President Putin's view of Donald Trump as someone who aligns with authoritarianism and a desire for a positive relationship with Russia, concluding 'he's one of us'.

Social Media Montage.description: A rapid, layered sequence of social media posts, news headlines, and images, including tweets, Facebook posts, and articles with sensationalized titles about Hillary Clinton, WikiLeaks, and political conspiracies, displayed in a chaotic, overlapping format, which is described by a narrator as containing 'misinformation,' 'fake users designed to look like Americans,' 'trolls supporting the Kremlin,' 'an army of automated bots on Twitter,' and 'targeted advertisements on Facebook and Google'.

Golden Curtain Backdrop.description: A rich, ornate golden curtain with intricate patterns, serving as the background behind Donald Trump during his speech, adding a formal and regal atmosphere to the scene.

Donald Trump.description: A man with light-colored hair, wearing a dark suit and blue tie, speaking at a podium with American flags behind him, gesturing with his hands as he addresses the audience. He is discussed by voiceovers as failing to secure a second term and showing 'no signs that he's prepared to concede this election'. His voice is heard emphatically stating, 'Frankly, we did win this election!' amidst crowd cheers and applause.

Bill Clinton.description: A man with graying hair, wearing a dark suit and pink tie, shown in black-and-white footage standing beside Hillary Clinton, looking forward with a neutral expression.

Peter Baker.description: A man with dark hair and glasses, wearing a dark suit and light blue tie, speaking in a formal setting, identified by on-screen text as 'Co-author, The Divider'. His voice explains that from Putin's perspective, he won the election and disrupted American democracy, making the US too distracted to pose a threat.

Burning Vehicle.description: A vehicle engulfed in flames on a city street, with thick black smoke rising, surrounded by onlookers and emergency vehicles, with protest sounds like sirens in the background.

Melania Trump.description: A woman in a beige coat with a gold belt buckle, walking beside Donald Trump during a formal meeting, her expression composed and neutral.

The Kremlin.description: A large, red-brick fortress with towers and walls, visible in an aerial shot of Moscow, with a prominent star-topped spire in the foreground, indicating the location of the diplomatic setting. It is mentioned by a news anchor in the context of President Vladimir Putin.

John Bolton.description: A man with gray hair and a mustache, wearing glasses, a dark suit, light blue shirt, and red-striped tie, speaking in an interview with expressive hand gestures. He speaks with a distinct voice, offering analysis that 'in Trump, Putin had a very different American president,' and that Trump 'never understood what the nature of Putin's game was,' leading Putin to believe Trump was 'a fool and easily manipulable'.

Protesters.description: A diverse crowd of civilians, many wearing winter clothing and holding signs such as 'COUNT EVERY LEGAL VOTE' and 'TRUMP 2020,' marching down a street toward the U.S. Capitol, with American flags and banners visible. Their presence is accompanied by sounds of crowd commotion, and they are part of a scene described by voiceovers as 'chaos,' 'division,' 'contested election,' and 'violence'.

Satellite View Interface.description: A digital overlay showing Earth from space, zooming from Moscow, Russia, to a broader map highlighting Russia and Ukraine, with green targeting reticles, signal status bars, and location labels, suggesting a strategic or intelligence monitoring system.

Camera Operator.description: A person in the foreground holding a large professional camera, capturing the diplomatic meeting between Trump and Putin, with a visible lens and camera body.

Yamiche Alcindor.description: A woman with curly hair, wearing a green top, speaking directly to the camera in an interview setting. She provides commentary in a clear voice, describing Trump's eagerness and Putin's seemingly unhappy demeanor during the meeting, and reiterating Trump's view on relations with Russia.

Ornate Meeting Room.description: An opulent room with white walls, gold accents, and large flags, hosting a formal diplomatic meeting between Trump and Putin, featuring a small table with white flowers and ornate chairs.

Greg Miller.description: A man with short dark hair, wearing a dark suit and light blue shirt, speaking in a dimly lit setting, identified by on-screen text as 'Greg Miller, The Washington Post'.

Eugene Robinson.description: A man with glasses and a mustache, wearing a dark blazer over a blue shirt, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit indoor setting, identified by on-screen text as 'Eugene Robinson, The Washington Post', whose voice is heard commenting on the 'starkest possible way' a question was put to the president.

Reporter with Microphone.description: A man in a dark suit holding a microphone, standing in the audience during the press conference, raising it to ask a question to Donald Trump, his voice clearly heard asking 'who do you believe?' regarding Russian election interference.

Press Conference Setup.description: An indoor stage with two podiums, American and Russian flags, microphones, and a sign reading 'HELSINKI 2018', surrounded by a seated audience of journalists and officials, with a large chandelier overhead.

Audience Members.description: Formally dressed individuals seated at tables in the conference hall, many wearing headphones for translation, including reporters holding microphones, listening intently to the leaders' statements, with commentators noting that "everybody else in the room" and "everybody who was in the room with them" witnessed the events.

Earth from Space.description: A green-tinted, pixelated view of Earth from orbit, with targeting graphics and a signal status bar, transitioning into a zoomed-in satellite view of Washington, D.C.

Signal Status Display.description: A green digital overlay in the top right corner of the screen showing a 'SIGNAL STATUS' bar with fluctuating levels, indicating a surveillance or monitoring system interface.

Susan Glasser.description: A woman with short brown hair, wearing a red blazer and patterned scarf, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit interview setting, identified by on-screen text as 'Co-author, The Divider'. Her voice is heard throughout the segment, providing commentary on Donald Trump's relationship with Vladimir Putin and his stance on Ukraine and Crimea.

Joe Biden.description: A man with white hair, shown in a color graphic with a blue suit and tie, smiling, with the text 'PRESIDENT ELECT JOE BIDEN' displayed prominently, indicating his election victory. He is projected as the winner by Fox News anchors, who refer to him as 'former Vice President Joe Biden' and 'Joseph Robinette Biden'.

NBC News Graphic.description: A vibrant, color graphic with a blue and white background, displaying 'PRESIDENT ELECT JOE BIDEN' in bold text, with a checkmark and the NBC News logo in the corner.

Marie Yovanovitch.description: A woman with gray hair and glasses, wearing a dark blazer and a gold necklace, speaking earnestly in a dimly lit room, identified by on-screen text as 'U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine, 2016–19,' gesturing with her hands as she speaks. Her voice is heard explaining how Putin gained from President Trump's policies and anticipated his re-election.

Black Presidential Limousine.description: A long, black armored vehicle with American and Russian flags mounted on the front, driving through a city street, surrounded by a crowd of people, some holding flags and signs, appearing amidst a scene described by voiceovers as 'chaos' and 'violence'.

Fox News Anchors.description: Two news anchors, a man in a dark suit with a striped tie and a woman in a green dress, seated at a glass desk in a studio with a cityscape backdrop, appearing composed and professional. Their voices are heard projecting Joe Biden as the winner of the election.

Fox News Studio.description: A modern news studio with a large glass desk, two anchors, and a cityscape backdrop visible through large windows, featuring the 'FOX NEWS' logo in the corner.

raw_segments: The video opens with a sweeping aerial time-lapse of Washington, D.C., transitioning from late afternoon to dusk, showcasing the city’s iconic skyline including the Washington Monument and the U.S. Capitol, with traffic flowing along highways and the Potomac River reflecting the fading light. Upbeat, slightly tense background music begins as a female news anchor states, "The 2016 presidential race is underway." A male narrator adds, "Having tested the United States' resolve, Putin would go even further." The scene shifts to a ground-level view of the Washington Monument at sunset, framed by bare tree branches, followed by a dramatic twilight shot of the Capitol Building as the sky turns deep purple and pink, with its dome illuminated. The news anchor continues, "And the candidates are once again fanned out across..." and the narrator interjects, "Striking at the heart of American democracy." The anchor mentions, "Candidates make a final push to shore up..." before the narrator concludes, "Interfering in the 2016 presidential election," with the anchor adding, "Just a few days away." The sequence then cuts to an interview with Fiona Hill, identified as 'Nat'l. Security Council, 2017–19,' wearing a red collared shirt and speaking expressively in a dimly lit indoor setting. She explains in a clear, articulate voice with a slight accent, "There's two goals there: discredit our election, and also to discredit the presidency, to neutralize the United States as an adversary, to really tarnish US leadership as, you know, the leader of the democratic world, the free world, the West." The FRONTLINE logo is visible. This is followed by a night-time time-lapse of the city’s highways, with light trails from vehicles creating dynamic streaks, accompanied by the continuing background music. The video then transitions to a close-up of blinking yellow lights on server hardware, suggesting digital infrastructure, before showing a screen of WikiLeaks with the text 'Search the DNC email data' and multiple overlapping email documents. The male narrator's voice returns, stating, "Putin's attack was aimed at Washington D.C. Russian hackers breaching Democratic Party computer servers. Email leaks designed to undermine Hillary Clinton's presidential campaign." The final segment features an interview with James Clapper, identified as 'Dir. of National Intelligence, 2010–17,' wearing a dark suit and patterned tie, speaking with serious, deliberate hand gestures in a dimly lit room. He asserts in a deep, serious voice, "This was the most aggressive, most direct, and most assertive campaign that the Russians ever mounted in the history of our elections. And..." The FRONTLINE logo appears in the bottom left corner during both interviews.

The minute opens with a close-up of server hardware, its blinking green lights and tangled cables suggesting a digital or cyber context, while a male voice (later identified as Stephen Hadley) discusses the "variety and intensity of the techniques that they employed." This transitions into a rapid montage of social media posts and news headlines, many referencing 'DCLeaks,' Hillary Clinton, and conspiracy theories, including claims about FBI agents, WikiLeaks, and political scandals, with images of Hillary Clinton and Bill Clinton, tweets, and sensationalized articles. A narrator's voice, accompanied by a low, suspenseful hum, details "misinformation," "fake users designed to look like Americans," "trolls supporting the Kremlin," "an army of automated bots on Twitter," and "targeted advertisements on Facebook and Google," all "calculated to hurt Clinton and help her rival, Donald Trump." The scene shifts to black-and-white footage of Hillary Clinton speaking at a podium, followed by Donald Trump standing solemnly beside her, both in front of a backdrop of the U.S. Constitution. An interview with Stephen Hadley, identified as National Security Adviser 2005–09, is then shown in a dimly lit room with a 'FRONTLINE' logo, where he explains "President Putin's view of Trump was a pension for authoritarianism, backing off of human rights, and talking about wanting to have a more positive relationship with Russia," asking, "What's not to like if you're Vladimir Putin?" and concluding, "he's one of us." This is followed by a black-and-white close-up of Vladimir Putin, his face dramatically lit, conveying intensity. The scene changes to a bright, colorful daytime shot of the White House, with a fountain and vibrant red tulips in the foreground. As news anchors announce "Donald Trump pulled off one of the biggest political upsets in America," and declare "a victory for Trump and for Putin," stating "Donald Trump is elected the 45th President," and "shocking the nation and the world," the final shot shows Donald Trump speaking at a podium with the presidential seal, an American flag behind him, and a golden curtain backdrop, gesturing emphatically.

The video opens with Donald Trump, in a dark suit and red tie, speaking emphatically at a podium with an American flag and golden curtains behind him, his voice heard stating he was congratulated on winning the election. The scene then cuts to Peter Baker, co-author of 'The Divider,' speaking in a dimly lit room, explaining that from Putin's perspective, the favored candidate won and disrupted America's faith in democracy. As Baker speaks, the visuals shift to an urban protest with demonstrators holding 'FIGHT TRUMP' and 'AGAINST HATE' signs, accompanied by audible crowd noise and sirens. This transitions to a chaotic street scene featuring a burning vehicle and smoke, while Baker's voice concludes that America is too distracted to pose a threat to Putin. A dramatic, suspenseful musical score begins as a black-and-white close-up of Vladimir Putin, dramatically lit, appears. The scene then shows a green-tinted satellite view of The White House, overlaid with targeting graphics, before zooming into a similar view of The Kremlin, as a female news anchor mentions 'President Trump's' and 'President Vladimir Putin.' Trump and Putin are then seen walking side-by-side on a red carpet, followed by Melania Trump, and posing for a photo, while a male narrator states that Vladimir Putin would confront a new American president for the fourth time, and another male news anchor urges confronting Putin about Russia's 2016 election interference. The video cuts to John Bolton, National Security Adviser 2018–19, speaking in an interview, his voice describing Vladimir Putin as 'clear-eyed and cold-blooded' and relentlessly pursuing Russia's national interest. The segment concludes with Trump and Putin seated for a diplomatic meeting in an ornate room with American and Russian flags, surrounded by aides and photographers, including a camera operator in the foreground, as Bolton's voice fades and the dramatic music continues.

The segment opens with close-ups of Donald Trump, in a dark suit and red tie, and Vladimir Putin, in a dark suit and patterned tie, both seated before American and Russian flags. A female voice, identified as Yamiche Alcindor, describes Trump as sitting "up in his chair looking as though he really, really wants to get something out of this meeting." The scene transitions to a medium shot of the leaders in an ornate room with flags and white flowers, where Trump gestures as he speaks. Trump's distinct voice is heard congratulating Putin, saying, "Well, first of all, Mr. President, I'd like to congratulate you on a really great World Cup." A subtle "whoosh" sound and background chatter are present during this exchange. Alcindor's voice then notes Putin is "kind of slumped in his chair, not smiling, doesn't look like he's having a good time," as Trump's voice continues, asserting that "getting along with Russia is a good thing, not a bad thing." The video briefly shows Trump and Putin shaking hands before returning to their seated positions. An interview with John Bolton, wearing a dark suit and striped tie, follows, where he speaks with expressive hand gestures. Bolton's voice provides analysis, stating, "It was clear that in Trump, Putin had a very different American president," and that "Trump never understood... what the nature of Putin's game was." He suggests Putin "thought Trump was a fool and easily manipulable." The segment concludes with Putin seated alone in the ornate meeting room as aides pass by, and Bolton's voice remarks, "The cameras were sent away."

The segment opens with a black-and-white scene of Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin seated across from each other in a formal meeting room, flanked by American and Russian flags, with a small table holding white flowers and papers between them. A close-up on Putin reveals his neutral, contemplative expression, as a male narrator with a deep voice explains, accompanied by ominous, low background music, that "For two hours, they met privately, without any aides." The narrator continues, stating that "Putin didn't need the KGB's psychological profilers to hand him a dossier on how to play Donald Trump," and that "He was able to read Trump... He knew how to play Trump like a violin." The scene transitions to a color interview with Eugene Robinson, identified by on-screen text, a man with glasses and a mustache wearing a dark blazer over a blue shirt, speaking directly to the camera. The video then cuts back to black-and-white footage of Trump and Putin shaking hands, followed by a color shot of them walking together through a grand hallway, surrounded by aides. The setting shifts to a large, opulent hall with chandeliers and flags, where they stand at podiums labeled 'HELSINKI 2018' during a joint press conference, facing a seated audience of journalists and officials. The narrator states, "Afterwards, when they faced the press, a shocking statement." A reporter in a dark suit raises a microphone, asking in a clear voice, "President Putin denied having anything to do with the election interference in 2016. Every US intelligence agency has concluded that Russia did. My first question for you, sir, is, who do you believe?" Another voice echoes, "Who do you believe?" Eugene Robinson then comments, "That's the starkest possible way to put that question to the president." Trump is shown speaking at the Helsinki 2018 podium, with American and Russian flags behind him, maintaining a serious demeanor, his distinct voice heard stating, "My people came to me, Dan Coats came to me and some others, they said, they think it's Russia. Uh, I have uh President..." The segment includes an interview with Greg Miller of The Washington Post, identified by on-screen text, speaking in a dimly lit setting, before concluding with Trump continuing his speech at the Helsinki 2018 podium.

The video opens with Donald Trump, in a dark suit and red tie, speaking at a podium labeled 'HELSINKI 2018', flanked by American and Russian flags. His voice is heard stating, "Putin, he just said it's not Russia. I will say this, I don't see any reason why it would be." The camera then cuts to a wider shot showing both Trump and Vladimir Putin at separate podiums, Putin calmly dressed in a dark suit. The scene alternates between close-ups of Trump speaking, where he is heard describing President Putin as "extremely strong and powerful in his denial today," and Putin listening or speaking with a composed expression. An audience of reporters and officials is visible in the foreground, with a male commentator's voice expressing disbelief, "I felt frozen to my chair like everybody else in the room, we couldn't believe it." A female commentator's voice describes how "everybody who was in the room with them just saw how masterfully Putin manipulated Trump," further adding that Trump was seen as "gullible and stupid and easily manipulatable," and that Putin was "just making ropes out of him, just braiding him like a braid." After the speeches, Trump and Putin shake hands and walk off stage. The scene transitions to a digital satellite interface with green targeting graphics, zooming from Earth to 'THE WHITE HOUSE, WASHINGTON, D.C.', as a male commentator calls it an "extraordinary moment in American history" and a "disgraceful play by the President of the United States as he stood by Vladimir Putin." The satellite view gives way to a blurred, fast-moving shot of the White House grounds with American flags waving, ending in a chaotic park view, while commentators discuss how "concern continues to grow about what they said to each other behind closed doors," even calling it a "bromance."

The minute opens with a blurred, fast-moving shot of a park-like setting, possibly near the White House, featuring a fountain and trees, set to a serious, investigative background music. Susan Glasser, identified as 'Co-author, The Divider,' appears in a dimly lit interview, wearing a red blazer and patterned scarf, her voice heard discussing a "Trump romance" with Putin and how "Trump acted in some ways as an arm of Putin's foreign policy." Multiple male narrators then describe "President Trump on the attack, asking what good is NATO," and "questioning the entire point to one of America's oldest alliances," further stating that "Trump was echoing Putin's talking points on the key battleground of Ukraine," and that "Ukraine is one of the most corrupt countries in the world." Glasser's voice reinforces this, saying, "He would say Ukraine is a really corrupt country." The visual transitions to dynamic, handheld clips of Donald Trump interacting with reporters outdoors, surrounded by microphones and cameras, gesturing emphatically and speaking animatedly in a dark suit with either a red or blue tie. Close-ups of his hands and face are shown, along with wider shots of him walking toward a helicopter on the White House lawn. During these visuals, a female narrator states, "The President is saying that Crimea probably should belong to Russia because everybody there speaks Russian," a point Glasser's voice reiterates, "Crimea is Russian. Everybody there speaks Russian and wants to be part of Russia." A male narrator then explicitly states, "Donald Trump parroting Vladimir Putin, saying that Ukraine is not even a real country." Glasser's voice questions, "Where did he hear these things? He heard them from Putin," and adds, "You know, and he would just echo them." Another male narrator concludes that "Trump is actually helping Putin reconstitute the Soviet Union," to which Glasser's voice responds, "It was just too easy." The segment concludes with a black-and-white close-up of Vladimir Putin, seated in an ornate chair with a serious, slightly smirking expression, as the narrator's voice is cut off mid-sentence.

The segment opens with a series of black-and-white close-ups of Vladimir Putin, seated in an ornate chair, his expression shifting from serious and slightly smirking to contemplative, then an intense stare. Over these visuals, a male voiceover discusses the pointlessness of continuing to punish Russia. This transitions to color interview footage of Marie Yovanovitch, identified by on-screen text as 'U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine, 2016–19,' speaking earnestly in a dimly lit room, wearing glasses, a dark blazer, and a gold necklace, gesturing with her hands. Her voice is heard stating, "He's getting pretty much everything he needs from President Trump," detailing how Putin is "getting the smackdown of NATO, the humiliation of our best allies," and that "the list goes on and on and on." Yovanovitch continues, explaining that "Putin was probably betting on a second term of President Trump and hoping that he could get his way on many things, whether it's NATO, whether it's Ukraine, without using the instrument of war." The scene then shifts to a Fox News studio with two anchors at a desk. A male voiceover states, "But Trump did not get a second term." The Fox News anchors, a man and a woman, are seen as the male anchor announces, "The Fox News decision desk can now project that former Vice President Joe Biden..." A glitchy, colorful transition then displays an NBC News graphic declaring 'PRESIDENT ELECT JOE BIDEN.' Another male voiceover adds, "And with a new president..." followed by the female Fox News anchor completing the projection, "elect Joseph Robinette Biden." Donald Trump is then shown speaking at a podium with American flags behind him, his voice heard declaring, "Frankly, we did win this election!" accompanied by crowd cheers and applause. A male voiceover describes "an escalation of the chaos in America that Putin helped foment." The video then shows a chaotic street scene with a black presidential limousine, adorned with American and Russian flags, surrounded by a crowd. A voiceover mentions, "The president showing no signs that he's prepared to concede this election..." followed by another voice stating, "Division. This is a contested election. Many people are nervous..." and finally, "Violence." The segment concludes with a large protest march toward the U.S. Capitol, with demonstrators holding signs like 'COUNT EVERY LEGAL VOTE' and 'TRUMP 2020,' under a clear sky, as sounds of a crowd and general commotion continue.

event_blocks.summary: Chaotic, handheld footage at night near the U.S. Capitol Building captured police in riot gear arresting protesters amidst sounds of struggle, shouting, and sirens, with one voice declaring "insurrection." The scene shifted to daytime, wide-angle shots of the U.S. Capitol Building under siege by a massive crowd of protesters, many wearing red hats and waving 'TRUMP 2020' flags, who breached the exterior and climbed its steps and walls, while inside, protesters surged through arched doorways. Timothy Snyder stated, "Nobody loved January 6th more than the Russians did," calling it "the wrapping, the beautiful wrapping, you know, the package" for the "four years" Trump gave them, and asserted that "January 6th, apart from anything else, leads directly to the war in Ukraine" because it made America "look like America is weak." Bodycam footage from January 6, 2021, showed police in riot gear being overwhelmed by protesters, with one officer pushed to the ground amid continued sounds of struggle. A black-and-white close-up of Vladimir Putin with a stern expression preceded a green-tinted satellite view interface zooming from the White House to Geneva, Switzerland, with targeting graphics, as a news report announced a "historic meeting with Russian President Vladimir... soon in Geneva," where "Vladimir Putin faced his old nemesis, President Biden," noting "for neither of them is this their first rodeo. The tension seemed to be a bit high." In June 2021, Joe Biden and Vladimir Putin shook hands on a red carpet in Geneva, flanked by U.S. and Russian flags, with Biden placing a hand on Putin’s arm as they walked into a building. Evan Osnos explained that "Putin is looking at Biden and saying, 'This is a weak American president. He doesn't have the overwhelming support of his public. His people are so divided.'" The segment then showed Biden and Putin shaking hands, with Biden smiling and Putin maintaining a neutral expression, as they entered a grand library flanked by American and Russian flags, sitting across from each other with a floral arrangement and large globe between them. A narrator noted the need to 'hold Biden off long enough so that he can continue to assemble the plan that he's putting together,' describing them as 'children of the Cold War' and stating 'Biden saw Putin for what he is, a KGB revanchist who wants to remake the Russian Empire.' Antony Blinken, identified as 'now Secretary of State,' took notes to Biden’s left, and another aide to Putin’s right, as the first narrator described their 'body language' as 'icy,' emphasizing that 'these are two people who do not like each other.' The second narrator concluded that the conversation's tone was 'professional' and 'direct,' but 'President Biden made very clear where we saw things differently, and so did President Putin,' underscoring clear disagreements despite the formal setting under warm, consistent lighting.

Timothy Snyder.description: A man with graying hair, wearing a light blue button-down shirt, speaking earnestly in a color interview, identified by on-screen text as 'Author, The Road to Unfreedom,' who states that "Nobody loved January 6th more than the Russians did," and that January 6th "leads directly to the war in Ukraine" because "America is weak."

Joe Biden.description: A man with white hair, wearing a dark suit and blue tie, seated in a wooden chair, displaying a serious expression and occasionally gesturing during the meeting with Vladimir Putin. A narrator states he saw Putin as a 'KGB revanchist,' and during the professional yet direct conversation, he 'made very clear where we saw things differently.'

FRONTLINE Logo.description: A red rectangular logo with white text reading 'FRONTLINE' in the bottom left corner of the screen, indicating the documentary series producing the segment, visible during Timothy Snyder's and Evan Osnos's interviews.

Satellite View Interface.description: A green-tinted digital overlay showing Earth from space, zooming from the White House to Geneva, Switzerland, with targeting graphics, signal status bars, and location labels, suggesting a strategic or intelligence monitoring system.

Bodycam Footage.description: First-person footage from a police officer’s body camera, timestamped '2021-01-06 14:28:37 -0500', showing police in riot gear being overwhelmed by protesters, with one officer being pushed to the ground.

Vladimir Putin.description: A man with a receding hairline, wearing a dark suit and patterned purple tie, seated across from Biden, maintaining a composed and intense demeanor throughout the discussion. A narrator describes him as a 'KGB revanchist who wants to remake the Russian Empire,' and he also 'made very clear where we saw things differently' during the professional yet direct conversation.

Police in Riot Gear.description: Uniformed officers in dark tactical gear, helmets, and shields, actively arresting protesters in a chaotic street scene, with visible force and movement, including one officer being pushed to the ground in bodycam footage, accompanied by sounds of struggle and sirens.

U.S. Capitol Building.description: A grand neoclassical building with a large dome, shown under siege by a massive crowd of protesters during the day, with people climbing its steps and walls, and later seen from the interior as protesters surge through arched doorways.

Evan Osnos.description: A man with light brown hair, wearing a dark suit and light blue tie, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit room, identified by on-screen text as 'Author, Joe Biden,' who analyzes Putin's perspective, stating that Putin views Biden as a "weak American president" because "he doesn't have the overwhelming support of his public" and "his people are so divided."

Protesters.description: A diverse crowd of civilians, many wearing winter clothing and holding 'TRUMP 2020' flags and American flags, breaching the U.S. Capitol Building, climbing its steps and walls, and surging through its interior corridors, heard shouting and struggling, and described as part of an "insurrection."

Aide with Notepad.description: A man in a dark suit, seated beside Vladimir Putin, holding a notepad and pen, actively taking notes during the diplomatic discussion, indicating his role as a personal assistant or advisor.

Antony Blinken.description: A man with graying hair, wearing a dark suit and blue tie, seated to Biden’s left, taking notes during the meeting, his expression focused and attentive, identified by a narrator as 'now Secretary of State.'

raw_segments: The segment opens with chaotic, handheld footage at night, showing police in riot gear arresting protesters near the U.S. Capitol amidst sounds of struggle, shouting, and sirens, as a voice declares "insurrection." The scene quickly shifts to daytime, wide-angle shots of the U.S. Capitol Building under siege by a massive crowd of protesters, many wearing red hats and waving 'TRUMP 2020' flags, breaching the exterior and climbing its steps and walls, while inside, chaos continues as protesters surge through arched doorways. Over these visuals, Timothy Snyder, identified as 'Author, The Road to Unfreedom,' states that "Nobody loved January 6th more than the Russians did," explaining that "Trump gave them four years, which was one big gift. But January 6th was like the wrapping, the beautiful wrapping, you know, the package. January 6th, apart from anything else, leads directly to the war in Ukraine. Because it looks like America is not just morally discredited, looks like America is weak." This is followed by bodycam footage from January 6, 2021, showing police in riot gear being overwhelmed by protesters, with one officer pushed to the ground, accompanied by continued sounds of struggle. The video then transitions to a black-and-white close-up of Vladimir Putin with a stern expression, followed by a green-tinted satellite view interface zooming from the White House to Geneva, Switzerland, with targeting graphics. A news report voiceover announces a "historic meeting with Russian President Vladimir... soon in Geneva," where "Vladimir Putin faced his old nemesis, President Biden," noting that "for neither of them is this their first rodeo. The tension seemed to be a bit high." The scene shifts to June 2021, showing Joe Biden and Vladimir Putin shaking hands on a red carpet in Geneva, flanked by U.S. and Russian flags, with Biden placing a hand on Putin’s arm as they walk into a building. Evan Osnos, identified as 'Author, Joe Biden,' then explains that "Putin is looking at Biden and saying, 'This is a weak American president. He doesn't have the overwhelming support of his public. His people are so divided.'" The final shot returns to Biden and Putin shaking hands, with Biden smiling and Putin maintaining a neutral expression, as they enter the building together, as Osnos's commentary concludes.

The segment opens with Joe Biden, in a dark suit and blue tie, shaking hands with Vladimir Putin, in a dark suit and patterned purple tie, as they enter a grand library flanked by American and Russian flags. They then sit across from each other in ornate wooden chairs, a floral arrangement and large globe on the table between them, against a backdrop of tall bookshelves. A male narrator's voice immediately sets a strategic tone, noting the need to 'hold Biden off long enough so that he can continue to assemble the plan that he's putting together.' The camera alternates between close-ups of their serious expressions—Biden occasionally adjusting, Putin maintaining a composed, steady gaze—as the narrator describes them as 'children of the Cold War,' adding that 'Biden saw Putin for what he is, a KGB revanchist who wants to remake the Russian Empire.' A brief black-and-white interlude shows a man writing notes. The scene returns to color, now including Antony Blinken, identified by another narrator as 'now Secretary of State,' taking notes to Biden’s left, and another aide to Putin’s right. The first narrator describes their 'body language' as 'icy,' emphasizing that 'these are two people who do not like each other.' The second narrator concludes by stating the conversation's tone was 'professional' and 'direct,' but 'President Biden made very clear where we saw things differently, and so did President Putin,' underscoring the clear disagreements despite the formal setting. The warm, consistent lighting emphasizes the gravity of this tense, high-stakes diplomatic encounter.

event_blocks.summary: President Joe Biden, in a formal meeting with Vladimir Putin, emphasized a commitment to Ukraine's sovereignty and territorial integrity, a key priority for deterring Russian aggression. Despite Biden's clear warnings during a subsequent video conference about severe costs and consequences for any invasion, Putin responded with denial, claiming no imminent aggression against Ukraine. However, U.S. officials, including Secretary of State Antony Blinken and former Ambassador to Ukraine Marie Yovanovitch, possessed extraordinary visibility into Russia's plans, recognizing the "storm rising" and the "heavy military equipment clearly taking an aggressive posture" not just at the eastern border but also near Ukraine's borders with Belarus and Russia, as confirmed by satellite images showing increased troop presence and movement. Yevgenia Albats, a Russian journalist, noted Putin's understanding of the West's perceived inability to make decisive steps and his belief that there would be no significant consequences. For two decades, Putin had defied American presidents, with actions including invading Georgia, seizing Crimea, and interfering in elections, and he continued to push forward with the invasion, defying Biden's warnings. Putin's calculation, according to Yovanovitch, was that Biden would not be able to lead an international coalition to support Ukraine, underestimating the international will to continue that support.

Joe Biden.description: A man with white hair, wearing a dark suit and tie, seen seated contemplatively at a long table with American flags, and later in profile during a black-and-white video conference with Putin, where he is described by a narrator as warning Putin to 'pull back from the brink'.

Russian Presidential Aircraft.description: A large white aircraft with 'РОССИЯ' written on the fuselage and Russian flag on the tail, shown taxiing and taking off from a runway.

Vladimir Putin.description: A man with a receding hairline, wearing a dark suit and patterned tie, seated at a formal table or in ornate chairs, maintaining a composed and intense demeanor throughout the segment, shown in black-and-white footage with Russian flags and formal settings. He is referred to by the male narrator as having "ignored Biden and pushed forward with the invasion," and as having "defied American presidents" for two decades, including "Invading Georgia, seizing Crimea, interfering in a presidential election," and that "he wasn't stopping now." Yevgenia Albats and Marie Yovanovitch also refer to him and his strategic calculations.

William Taylor.description: A man with graying hair and glasses, wearing a dark suit and patterned tie, speaking in a dimly lit room, identified by on-screen text as 'U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine, 2006–09', whose voice is heard describing 'heavy military equipment clearly taking an aggressive posture' near Ukraine's borders.

Military Tanks.description: Large armored vehicles with turrets, shown moving through snow-covered fields and along a snowy road, kicking up snow as they advance, accompanied by intense, dramatic music and Russian military commands, and described by William Taylor as 'heavy military equipment clearly taking an aggressive posture', representing 'Russian troops' and 'Russian forces' near Ukraine's border.

FRONTLINE.description: A red rectangular logo with white text reading 'FRONTLINE' in the bottom left corner of the screen, indicating the documentary series producing the segment, visible during William Taylor's interview.

Soldiers in White Winter Uniforms.description: Men in white winter military uniforms with red hats and white gloves, saluting in front of a hangar filled with armored vehicles in a snowy environment, accompanied by Russian military commands like 'Броня!' (Armor!) and 'Старт!' (Start!).

Julia Ioffe.description: A woman with shoulder-length brown hair, wearing a black and beige patterned top and gold hoop earrings, speaking in a dimly lit indoor setting, identified by on-screen text as 'Founding partner, Puck Media', whose voice is heard analyzing Putin's intentions and his desire to be a primary global problem and threat.

Antony Blinken.description: A man with graying hair, wearing a dark suit and blue tie, speaking in a color interview segment with an American flag behind him, identified by on-screen text as 'Secretary of State.' He has a clear, authoritative voice, explaining Biden's warning to Putin about 'severe' consequences and later detailing Putin's denial of aggression against Ukraine, emphasizing their real-time knowledge of contradictions.

Satellite View Interface.description: A green-tinted digital overlay showing Earth from space, zooming from Ukraine to military bases in Russia and Crimea, with targeting graphics, signal status bars, and location labels, suggesting a strategic or intelligence monitoring system. Multiple male narrators describe 'Russian troops surrounding Ukraine' and 'satellite images show an increase in Russian forces on the Ukraine border,' directly correlating with the visual information.

Marie Yovanovitch.description: A woman with gray hair and glasses, wearing a dark blazer and a gold necklace, speaking in a dimly lit room, identified by on-screen text as 'U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine, 2016–19', gesturing with her hands as she speaks. She speaks with a clear, measured voice, providing analysis of Putin's strategic calculations regarding international support for Ukraine.

Signal Status Display.description: A green digital overlay in the top right corner of the screen showing a 'SIGNAL STATUS' bar with fluctuating levels, indicating a surveillance or monitoring system interface.

Yevgenia Albats.description: A woman with curly hair and glasses, wearing a red collared shirt under a black jacket, speaking in a color interview setting, identified by on-screen text as 'Russian journalist' and 'FRONTLINE'. She speaks with a Russian accent, analyzing Putin's understanding of Western capabilities and his disregard for warnings from President Biden.

George W. Bush.description: A man with graying hair, wearing a dark suit and tie, shown in black-and-white archival footage walking beside Putin and later seated in ornate chairs during a formal meeting, mentioned by the narrator as one of the American presidents Putin defied.

Donald Trump.description: A man with light-colored hair, wearing a dark suit and tie, shown in black-and-white footage shaking hands with Putin in front of an American flag, mentioned by the narrator as one of the American presidents Putin defied.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a formal diplomatic meeting between Joe Biden and Vladimir Putin, seated across from each other in an ornate library setting, flanked by the U.S. and Russian flags, with Antony Blinken taking notes. A male narrator states that a "top priority for Biden" was "deterring Putin in Ukraine," followed by another male voice explaining that "President Biden made clear in the meeting to President Putin, our commitment to Ukraine, our commitment to its sovereignty, to its independence, to its territorial integrity." The scene transitions to an exterior shot of a grand building with a red carpet and flags, where Putin and his entourage exit and approach a black limousine. Julia Ioffe, a woman with shoulder-length brown hair, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit room, is heard explaining that "they wanted to park Putin but he didn't want to be parked," and that "he wanted to be the number one problem of everybody in the whole world," especially "the number one priority, the number one threat, the number one problem that the White House was dealing with." The footage then cuts to a Russian presidential aircraft, marked 'РОССИЯ', taking off from a runway. This is followed by a series of military scenes: soldiers in white winter uniforms saluting in front of a hangar filled with armored vehicles, and tanks moving through snowy fields and along a snow-covered road, all accompanied by intense, dramatic music and Russian military commands like "Броня!" and "Старт!". The narrator returns, stating that "Putin began laying the groundwork for the invasion of Ukraine," with another male voice adding that "100,000 Russian troops" were near Ukraine's border, "fueling fears" as "Russian forces start moving back toward Ukraine's borders." The segment concludes with William Taylor, identified as U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine (2006–09), speaking in an interview setting with the FRONTLINE logo visible, his voice describing "heavy military equipment clearly taking an aggressive posture," not just at the "eastern border."

The segment opens with a green-tinted satellite view interface, displaying a map of Eastern Europe with Ukraine highlighted, bordered by Belarus, Russia, and Crimea, while a male narrator describes "Russian troops surrounding Ukraine for days now," specifically mentioning the "Ukraine border with Belarus," "Ukraine border with Russia," and "Ukraine border with occupied Crimea." Another narrator adds that "satellite images show an increase in Russian forces on the Ukraine border," as a 'SIGNAL STATUS' bar is visible. The camera zooms into snowy military bases in Russia and Crimea, under a digital overlay, reinforcing the visual of troop movements. The scene transitions to a black-and-white meeting room where President Joe Biden, seated at the head of a long table with the Presidential Seal and American flags, listens intently with his hand on his chin. A narrator states that "an American president was facing a critical decision about how to deal with Putin." Antony Blinken, identified as 'Secretary of State' in a color interview, speaks with a serious tone, explaining that "it was in that moment, when a combination of seeing the forces deployed and then having from the information we'd gotten, real visibility on what Russia was actually thinking and what they were planning. That's when we really saw the storm rising and heading in Ukraine's direction." Finally, Marie Yovanovitch, identified as 'U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine, 2016–19,' speaks in a dimly lit room, gesturing as she warns, "If Russia is allowed to get away with this, if Russia stomps all over Ukraine and takes it over." Throughout, a serious, suspenseful orchestral background music underscores the geopolitical tension.

The segment opens with Marie Yovanovitch, identified as 'U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine, 2016–19,' speaking in a dimly lit color interview, wearing glasses and a dark blazer, her clear voice stating that the situation "affects the entire international order that we've been living under since World War II" and that "the stakes were pretty high and they continued to be very high." The scene transitions to black-and-white archival footage of Joe Biden, appearing contemplative at a long table with American flags, then in profile during a video conference with Vladimir Putin, whose image is on a large screen in a dark, formal room with Russian flags. A male narrator explains that "In a video conference with Putin, Biden warned him to pull back from the brink." Antony Blinken's voice then describes Biden's message, saying, "He's saying first of all, we see what you're doing, what you're planning, what you're plotting. And if you go ahead with what we're seeing, you need to understand the costs and consequences to Russia will be severe, and you need to know that, um, I mean what I say." The video cuts to a color interview with Antony Blinken, 'Secretary of State,' speaking directly to the camera with an American flag behind him. The narrator then states, "Putin's response was familiar. Denial," as dramatic, low-frequency music plays. The scene alternates between Blinken's interview and black-and-white close-ups of Putin, who gestures intensely. Blinken continues, explaining, "They're denying, uh, they're denying any imminent aggression against Ukraine. They're saying that that's not what we're doing." He concludes by stating, "But because we had such extraordinary visibility and extraordinary information, we knew in real time when Putin was saying something that was in contradiction with what was actually happening," as the final shot shows a serious, pensive Putin looking off to the side.

The segment opens with black-and-white footage of Vladimir Putin, serious and intense, seated at a long, reflective table with Russian flags, as a male narrator with a deep voice states that "Putin ignored Biden and pushed forward with the invasion." The scene cuts to Yevgenia Albats, a Russian journalist identified by on-screen text, who explains in a color interview, speaking with a Russian accent, that "Vladimir Putin understands the Western ability to make decisive steps pretty well," and that he knew there would be "not going to be any consequences" despite President Biden's warnings. The video returns to Putin, seen in side profile across from a screen displaying Joe Biden, followed by a close-up of Putin's stern gaze, as the narrator declares, "For two decades, Vladimir Putin had defied American presidents." Dramatic gunshot-like sound effects accompany archival black-and-white footage showing Putin with George W. Bush and Donald Trump, while the narrator lists his actions: "Invading Georgia, seizing Crimea, interfering in a presidential election." A high-contrast close-up of Putin's intense stare reinforces the narrator's assertion that "he wasn't stopping now." Marie Yovanovitch, identified as a former U.S. Ambassador to Ukraine, then speaks in a dimly lit room with a clear, measured voice, analyzing Putin's "calculation that Biden would not be able to lead an international group of countries to support Ukraine," and that "there wouldn't be the will to support Ukraine... it would not continue." The segment concludes with Putin seated in an ornate chair, hands clasped under his chin, maintaining a pensive and authoritative demeanor.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a stern, contemplative Vladimir Putin, hands clasped against a dark background with a Russian flag motif, as a narrator declares his pursuit of an all-out war to rebuild the Russian Empire, 30 years after the Soviet Union's fall. The scene shifts to a nighttime Ukrainian cityscape, punctuated by an orange glow on the horizon, a siren, and Putin's declaration of a 'special military operation,' followed by explosions and distant gunfire. Silhouettes of explosions and soldiers appear, a missile streaks across the daytime sky, and a building is engulfed in flames. An aerial view reveals a heavily damaged urban area with collapsed buildings and rubble, amidst continuous explosions, gunfire, and shellfire. Narrators describe 'apocalyptic scenes' on the outskirts of the capital, a 'non-stop onslaught' with mass casualties, and 'tens of thousands of civilians trapped' under Russian shellfire. A black-and-white profile of Putin reappears, before Julia Ioffe explains that Putin desires a pan-Slavic superstate with Moscow as the capital, viewing Ukraine as not a real country and leaving no room for compromise. The footage then shows chaotic scenes of a war-torn environment: a burning vehicle, a man running through debris, and a soldier in camouflage navigating a destroyed urban area with smoke and fire, as another narrator states Ukraine is calling it a 'full-scale invasion.' The conflict's human toll is emphasized by a man carrying a child through a damaged street, concluding the segment with the mention of the 'biggest war.'

Vladimir Putin.description: A man with a receding hairline, shown in black-and-white close-ups with intense, focused expressions, first in a formal seated pose and later in a dramatic side profile, illuminated by stark lighting against a dark background. His own voice is heard speaking in Russian, declaring a 'special military operation'.

Julia Ioffe.description: A woman with shoulder-length brown hair, wearing a black and beige patterned top and gold hoop earrings, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit indoor setting, identified by on-screen text as 'Founding partner, Puck Media'. Her clear female voice is heard discussing Putin's desire for Ukraine and a 'pan-Slavic superstate'.

War-Torn Urban Environment.description: A cityscape with heavily damaged buildings, rubble, and debris, shown in both aerial and ground-level views, conveying the aftermath of intense conflict and destruction, with smoke, fire, and debris visible throughout. This visual is underscored by sounds of explosions, gunfire, sirens, and shellfire, as narrators describe 'apocalyptic scenes' and 'constant Russian shellfire'.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a black-and-white close-up of Vladimir Putin, his expression stern and contemplative, hands clasped against a dark background with a faint Russian flag motif, as a somber, dramatic score begins. A male narrator states that "30 years after the fall of the Soviet Union, Vladimir Putin was now pursuing an all-out war to rebuild the Russian Empire." The scene transitions to a nighttime Ukrainian cityscape with illuminated buildings, then cuts to a dramatic orange glow on the horizon, suggesting an explosion or fire, accompanied by a siren. On-screen text reads 'I made a decision to carry out a special military operation,' which Putin himself declares in Russian, followed by sounds of explosions and distant gunfire. Dark, smoky silhouettes of explosions and soldiers appear in the distance, then a missile streaks across a daytime city sky, followed by a building engulfed in flames and smoke. An aerial shot reveals a heavily damaged urban area with collapsed buildings and rubble, all underscored by continuous explosions, gunfire, and shellfire. A narrator describes "apocalyptic scenes on the outskirts of the capital city" and a "non-stop onslaught over the last 24 hours with mass casualties," with "tens of thousands of civilians trapped in cities under near constant Russian shellfire." A black-and-white profile of Putin reappears, his face intensely illuminated from the side, before cutting to Julia Ioffe, identified as 'Founding partner, Puck Media,' speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit room. Her clear female voice explains, "Putin wants Ukraine. He wants this pan-Slavic superstate with Moscow as the capital... He doesn't think it's a real country. I don't know how you compromise with somebody who just wants Ukraine. That's not something you can build a bridge across." The video then shows chaotic, shaky footage of a war-torn environment: a burning vehicle, a man running through debris, and a soldier in camouflage moving through a destroyed urban area with smoke and fire. Another narrator states, "Ukraine is calling this a full-scale invasion," as the sounds of explosions and chaos intensify. The final frames depict a man carrying a child through a damaged street, emphasizing the human toll of the conflict, with the narrator concluding, "biggest war..."

event_blocks.summary: In a war-torn urban environment marked by damaged buildings, debris, and a smoke-filled sky, Joe Biden's voice echoed, referencing "Europe since World War II," as shaky footage revealed a desolate street with abandoned vehicles and a damaged van. Susan Glasser described the global anticipation of Vladimir Putin's troop movements into Ukraine, while the scene shifted to a dimly lit interior where traumatized civilians, including a woman in a floral dress and a child, huddled on the floor near a wheelchair, accompanied by the sound of a child crying. Biden questioned, "Will he stop?" as the battle for Ukraine commenced. The narrative then transitioned to a stark black-and-white office where Joe Biden, seated at an ornate desk with an American flag and presidential seal, spoke with expressive hand gestures, flanked by an aide, as a narrator detailed how Biden "went further than any president before, delivering weapons and intelligence to the front lines to help fight Putin." Susan Glasser, in a dimly lit interview setting, discussed the situation, followed by aerial combat footage depicting a snowy landscape with military vehicles, a massive explosion on a road with tanks, and multiple explosions in a field. Kori Schake, identified by on-screen text, stated in a color interview that "The United States has opted in to the fight," emphasizing the "enormous" and "staggering" scale of military support for Ukraine's defense, which Vladimir Putin "did not expect." A close-up profile of Vladimir Putin appeared as the narrator noted the swift arming of Ukraine by the US and NATO allies, with "Russian forces caught off guard, apparently in disarray," as Russia's anticipated swift invasion faltered when "their jets were plummeting from Ukrainian skies." The segment concluded with a return to the war-torn urban environment, showing a shattered glass view with a blurred vehicle, reinforcing the destruction, as Susan Glasser's voice concluded, "He clearly miscalculated the capabilities of the Russian military."

Joe Biden.description: A man with white hair, wearing a dark suit and striped tie, seated at an ornate desk in a black-and-white office setting, initially writing, then speaking with expressive hand gestures, flanked by an aide in a suit, with an American flag and presidential seal visible. He speaks with a clear, authoritative male voice, stating 'Europe since World War II' and asking 'Will he stop?'

Vladimir Putin.description: A man with a receding hairline, shown in a black-and-white close-up side profile, with intense, focused eyes, illuminated by stark lighting against a dark background, conveying seriousness and determination. His name is frequently mentioned by other speakers in the audio, but he does not speak himself.

Injured Civilian.description: A person lying motionless on the floor in a dimly lit interior, appearing traumatized, with a wheelchair nearby, highlighting the human toll of conflict. The visual is accompanied by the sound of a child crying.

Kori Schake.description: A woman with shoulder-length wavy brown hair, wearing a navy blazer with red lapels and a yellow-and-blue ribbon, speaking in a color interview, identified by on-screen text as 'Dir., American Enterprise Institute'. She speaks with a clear female voice, detailing how 'The United States has opted in to the fight. The amount and scale of the military support for Ukraine's defense... is enormous. It's staggering, clearly something that Vladimir Putin did not expect.'

Shattered Glass View.description: A close-up of cracked glass with spiderweb fractures, through which a blurred vehicle is visible, emphasizing destruction and vulnerability.

Susan Glasser.description: A woman with short brown hair, wearing a red blazer and patterned scarf, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit interview setting, identified by on-screen text as 'Co-author, The Divider'. She speaks with a clear female voice, describing 'The world is waiting to see just how far Putin's 190,000 troops will go into the country of Ukraine' and later concluding that 'He clearly miscalculated the capabilities of the Russian military'.

War-Torn Urban Environment.description: A cityscape with heavily damaged buildings, rubble, and debris, shown in both aerial and ground-level views, conveying the aftermath of intense conflict and destruction, with smoke, fire, and debris visible throughout. The scene is accompanied by the poignant sound of a child crying, emphasizing the human toll.

raw_segments: The video opens with chaotic, shaky footage of a war-torn urban environment, showing damaged buildings, debris, and a blackened, smoke-filled sky, suggesting recent bombardment, as a male voice (Joe Biden) states, "Europe since World War II." The camera pans across a desolate street with abandoned vehicles and a damaged van, emphasizing destruction, while a female voice (Susan Glasser) describes, "The world is waiting to see just how far Putin's 190,000 troops will go into the country of Ukraine." The scene shifts to a dimly lit interior where civilians, including a woman in a floral dress and a child, lie huddled on the floor, appearing traumatized and exhausted, with a wheelchair nearby, indicating displacement and suffering, underscored by the poignant sound of a child crying. Joe Biden's voice then asks, "Will he stop?" before a male narrator declares, "With the battle for Ukraine underway." The tone abruptly changes to a stark black-and-white office setting, where Joe Biden, a man with white hair in a dark suit and striped tie, is seated at a large ornate desk, writing intently, with an American flag and presidential seal visible. He then speaks with expressive hand gestures, flanked by an aide, as the narrator explains how "Biden went further than any president before, delivering weapons and intelligence to the front lines to help fight Putin." The scene cuts to Susan Glasser, a woman with short brown hair in a red blazer and patterned scarf, speaking directly to the camera in a dimly lit interview setting. The video then transitions to aerial combat footage: a snowy landscape with military vehicles, followed by a massive explosion on a road with tanks and armored vehicles, and then a wide shot of a field with multiple explosions and smoke trails. During this, Kori Schake, a woman with shoulder-length wavy brown hair, wearing a navy blazer with red lapels and a yellow-and-blue ribbon, speaks in a color interview, identified by on-screen text, stating, "The United States has opted in to the fight. The amount and scale of the military support for Ukraine's defense... is enormous. It's staggering, clearly something that Vladimir Putin did not expect." A close-up black-and-white profile of Vladimir Putin, intense and focused, appears as the narrator describes "The US and NATO allies are working quickly to arm Ukraine," and that "Russian forces caught off guard, apparently in disarray." The narrator continues, "Russia thought the invasion would be over swiftly. That was before their jets were plummeting from Ukrainian skies." The segment ends with a return to the war-torn urban environment, showing a shattered glass view with a blurred vehicle behind, reinforcing the theme of destruction and vulnerability, while Susan Glasser's voice concludes, "He clearly miscalculated the capabilities of the Russian military."

News Broadcasts.description: A montage of clips from CBS Mornings, ABC News, CNBC, and Fox News, each displaying breaking headlines about Putin’s actions, including troop mobilization, energy threats, and nuclear warnings, with on-screen banners and logos, featuring multiple male news anchors whose voices report on Ukraine's defiance, Putin's embarrassment, his preparation for a longer war, the call-up of 300,000 troops, economic warfare, and the threat of nuclear weapons.

Fox News Alert Banner.description: A red and white breaking news banner reading 'PUTIN THREATENING TO USE NUCLEAR WEAPONS' with the Fox News logo, displayed during a clip of Putin and soldiers in a field.

Rural Ukrainian Village.description: A cluster of modest houses in a rural setting, with smoke rising in the background, labeled 'CBS Mornings | BREAKING OVERNIGHT'.

Kremlin.description: A large, red-brick fortress with towers and walls, visible in an aerial shot of Moscow, with a prominent star-topped spire in the foreground, indicating the location of the diplomatic setting.

Soldiers in Camouflage.description: A group of soldiers in green camouflage uniforms, helmets, and balaclavas, carrying rifles and equipment, shown in both urban and rural settings, actively engaged in combat or patrol.

Vladimir Putin.description: Appears in black-and-white archival footage within a small inset video on the right side of the screen, shown in formal meetings with U.S. Presidents. His name is part of the program title 'Putin and the Presidents' mentioned by a female voiceover.

Explosion in Wooded Area.description: A violent blast in a green, forested environment, with smoke and debris, suggesting a military strike or bomb detonation.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Kori Schake, Director of the American Enterprise Institute, speaking in a close-up interview shot, wearing a navy blazer with red lapels and a yellow-and-blue ribbon supporting Ukraine. Her clear, articulate voice discusses the Ukrainian military's resolve and describes the conflict as a "miscalculation of Napoleonic magnitude." The scene abruptly shifts to a violent explosion in a green, wooded area, followed by a fiery blast engulfing a structure, suggesting a war zone, as a male news anchor declares, "Ukraine's president remains defiant." The anchor continues, noting "Ukraine's resolve has shocked the world and frustrated the Russian" forces, as the footage transitions to a serene view of the Kremlin, then a black-and-white close-up of Vladimir Putin with a stern expression. Another male voice reports that "Putin is facing the embarrassment of this offensive not necessarily being pulled off as smoothly as he" expected, as a montage of news clips begins. This is heard over a rural Ukrainian village under attack, protesters being arrested, and Putin seated at a desk, with news banners like 'CBS Mornings | BREAKING OVERNIGHT' and 'ABC News | PUTIN UNDER PRESSURE' reporting that "even as his army was pushed back, Putin was doubling down." A CNBC news anchor reports, "Coming out of Russia, where Vladimir Putin is preparing for a longer and more intense war on Ukraine," as Putin is shown on CNBC with the headline 'EUROPE FACES ENERGY CRISIS AS PUTIN THREATENS TOTAL ENERGY CUT-OFF', detailing that "President Putin suddenly announcing he would call up 300,000 men for military service." A glitchy transition to Fox News shows soldiers in camouflage as an anchor states, "Waging economic warfare," and quotes Putin: "No gas, no oil, nothing, he said, over a long winter." The segment concludes with Putin again on Fox News, reinforcing the nuclear threat, as the anchor warns of "raising the specter of nuclear annihilation," and "now threatening to use nuclear weapons," with a prominent 'PUTIN THREATENING TO USE NUCLEAR WEAPONS' banner.

The video opens with a Fox News breaking news segment, displaying Vladimir Putin in a formal setting with the Russian flag behind him, as red banners declare 'PUTIN THREATENS TO USE NUCLEAR WEAPONS' and 'FOX NEWS ALERT'. A male voice briefly states, "I'm not bluffing." The scene quickly transitions to Antony Blinken, Secretary of State, in a color interview, wearing a dark suit with a U.S. flag pin, speaking seriously with hand gestures against an American flag backdrop. Blinken, with a calm and analytical male voice, expresses deep concern over "Vladimir Putin doubling and tripling down on the aggression" through "mobilization," "the purported annexation of Ukrainian territory," and "loose talk about nuclear weapons," predicting this "is likely to continue for some time." The video then cuts to a stark black-and-white close-up of Vladimir Putin, his face dramatically illuminated, emphasizing his intense, focused gaze. This is followed by a similar black-and-white close-up of another man with a stern expression, likely a U.S. official. During these monochrome visuals, a deep, dramatic male narrator describes "Vladimir Putin, the former KGB agent, hell-bent on rebuilding the Russian Empire," now "a dangerous pariah on the world stage," "staring down yet another American president to see how far he will go." The sequence then shifts to a man in a suit and glasses, speaking directly to the camera in a color interview, his expression serious and engaged. With a clear, articulate male voice, he analyzes that "Putin is escalating into a war with the West more broadly," by "cutting off energy, threatening nuclear weapons," and that "part of the reason is to undercut Western resolve and to make the West eager to get this over with." The entire segment uses dramatic lighting and tight framing, alternating between color and monochrome, to heighten the tension and convey the urgency and gravity of the geopolitical narrative.

The segment opens with a dramatic black-and-white close-up of a man, identified by multiple speakers as Vladimir Putin, shown from behind in a dark suit with a white collared shirt, his receding hairline visible as he walks forward. He is silhouetted against a bright, gradient background, while various voices discuss his actions: one male voice mentions him cutting off natural gas supply amidst a "looming energy crisis," another male voice states he's "hoping that he can put pressure on the West to back off," and two female voices discuss him "counting on the fact that the Western powers are just going to lose interest in Ukraine" and questioning "how much longer the United States can continue to help bankroll this war." A third male voice asks if "Ukraine ultimately be left to fight this war on its own." As dark curtains with ornate designs part on either side, emphasizing a formal setting, a ticking sound begins, followed by a deep, ominous musical note as the scene fades to black. This transitions into a series of funding and production credits, displayed in white text over a dark, digital-style background with faint blurred imagery and horizontal light streaks, all set to dramatic, orchestral, and suspenseful music with a strong percussive, almost militaristic beat. The credits list sponsors like the Corporation for Public Broadcasting, PBS Viewers, Abrams Foundation, MacArthur Foundation, Park Foundation, Heising-Simons Foundation, and the FRONTLINE Journalism Fund (with its red rectangular logo). Production details for 'Putin and the Presidents' are shown, crediting Michael Kirk as director and Vanessa Fica as reporter, along with archival sources. The segment concludes with a male narrator stating, "For more on this and other Frontline programs, visit our website at pbs.org/frontline," as the URL pbs.org/frontline remains visible in the lower right corner.

The minute opens with rolling end credits for a FRONTLINE documentary, presented in white text against a dark, digital-style background featuring faint horizontal light streaks and blurred, abstract imagery. The red FRONTLINE logo remains consistently displayed in the top left corner. An upbeat, slightly dramatic orchestral-electronic instrumental score plays throughout this segment. On the right side of the screen, a small inset video appears, showcasing black-and-white archival footage of Vladimir Putin in formal meetings with U.S. Presidents George W. Bush and Donald Trump, accompanied by the title 'Putin and the Presidents' and an 'Available on prime video' promotion. At approximately 0:15, a female voiceover states, "Frontline's Putin and the Presidents is available on Amazon Prime Video," directly referencing the program and its availability. The credits continue to scroll, listing production roles, contributors like Michael Kirk and Vanessa Fica, and various funding sources including the Corporation for Public Broadcasting, Abrams Foundation, MacArthur Foundation, Park Foundation, Heising-Simons Foundation, and the FRONTLINE Journalism Fund. The URL PBS.org/frontline is also visible in the lower right corner. The instrumental music continues until around 0:41, when it transitions to a distinct, whimsical electronic chime. This chime accompanies the final visual, the purple, glowing GBH logo, before the screen fades to black.
